memberdef.definition: using Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::InsertResult = Dali::Pair<Value*, bool>
memberdef.name: InsertResult

briefdescription.para: Result type returned by TryInsert.

detaileddescription.para:
Equivalent to the std::pair<iterator, bool> returned by std::unordered_map::insert:
first : pointer to the value for the key (never null)
second : true if a new entry was inserted, false if the key already existed
Uses Dali::Pair so callers get the full Dali::Pair interface (comparison operators, Swap, MakePair) and the familiar first/second field names without depending on any bespoke type. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: constexpr uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::INITIAL_CAPACITY
memberdef.name: INITIAL_CAPACITY

memberdef.definition: Entry* Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mEntries
memberdef.name: mEntries

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mCapacity
memberdef.name: mCapacity

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mSize
memberdef.name: mSize

briefdescription.para: Live entries.

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mOccupied
memberdef.name: mOccupied

briefdescription.para: Live entries + tombstones (drives load-factor check).

memberdef.definition: DALI_NO_UNIQUE_ADDRESS Hash Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mHash
memberdef.name: mHash

memberdef.definition: DALI_NO_UNIQUE_ADDRESS KeyEqual Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::mKeyEqual
memberdef.name: mKeyEqual

memberdef.definition: Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::ManagedOpenHashMap
memberdef.name: ManagedOpenHashMap

briefdescription.para: Default constructor.

detaileddescription.para: Creates an empty map with no allocated table (capacity 0). The table is allocated on the first Insert. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::~ManagedOpenHashMap
memberdef.name: ~ManagedOpenHashMap

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: Destroys all live key-value pairs and frees the entry table. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::ManagedOpenHashMap
memberdef.name: ManagedOpenHashMap

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor is deleted.

detaileddescription.para: ManagedOpenHashMap is not copyable because copying a hash map with non-trivial key/value types is expensive and easily done unintentionally. Use move semantics or manually copy entries via ForEach() + Insert(). @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: ManagedOpenHashMap & Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::operator=
memberdef.name: operator=

briefdescription.para: Copy assignment operator is deleted.

detaileddescription.para: ManagedOpenHashMap is not copy-assignable for the same reason as the copy constructor. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::ManagedOpenHashMap
memberdef.name: ManagedOpenHashMap

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.

detaileddescription.para: Transfers ownership of the entry table from rhs to this map. After the move, rhs is in a valid empty state (size 0, capacity 0).
noexcept is conditional on Hash and KeyEqual being nothrow-move-constructible. If either functor's move constructor can throw, std::terminate would be called.

memberdef.definition: ManagedOpenHashMap & Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::operator=
memberdef.name: operator=

briefdescription.para: Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para: Frees this map's current entry table, then transfers ownership of the entry table from rhs. After the move, rhs is in a valid empty state (size 0, capacity 0).
noexcept is conditional on Hash and KeyEqual being nothrow-move-assignable.

simplesect.para: Reference to this map @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Insert
memberdef.name: Insert

briefdescription.para: Insert or update a key-value pair (copy both).

detaileddescription.para: WARNING: Unlike std::unordered_map::insert(), this always overwrites an existing value when the key is already present. Use TryInsert() for insert-if-absent semantics.
WARNING: Any pointer or iterator obtained before this call may be invalidated if the insert triggers a table grow.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the inserted/updated entry's value @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Insert
memberdef.name: Insert

briefdescription.para: Insert or update a key-value pair (move both).

detaileddescription.para: WARNING: Unlike std::unordered_map::insert(), this always overwrites an existing value when the key is already present. Use TryInsert() for insert-if-absent semantics.
WARNING: Any pointer or iterator obtained before this call may be invalidated if the insert triggers a table grow.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the inserted/updated entry's value @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Insert
memberdef.name: Insert

briefdescription.para: Insert or update a key-value pair (move key, copy value).

detaileddescription.para: WARNING: Unlike std::unordered_map::insert(), this always overwrites an existing value when the key is already present. Use TryInsert() for insert-if-absent semantics.
WARNING: Any pointer or iterator obtained before this call may be invalidated if the insert triggers a table grow.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the inserted/updated entry's value @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Insert
memberdef.name: Insert

briefdescription.para: Insert or update a key-value pair (copy key, move value).

detaileddescription.para: WARNING: Unlike std::unordered_map::insert(), this always overwrites an existing value when the key is already present. Use TryInsert() for insert-if-absent semantics.
WARNING: Any pointer or iterator obtained before this call may be invalidated if the insert triggers a table grow.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the inserted/updated entry's value @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value & Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::operator[]
memberdef.name: operator[]

briefdescription.para: Access or insert a value by key.

detaileddescription.para: If the key is present, returns a reference to the existing value unchanged. If the key is absent, inserts a default-constructed Value and returns a reference to it. This matches std::unordered_map::operator[] exactly.

Implementation note: routes through TryInsertImpl (not InsertImpl) so that an existing value is never overwritten by a default-constructed Value{}. Using InsertImpl here would silently reset an existing value to Value{} on every operator[] access for a key that is already present.
WARNING: Any reference obtained before this call may be invalidated if the call triggers a table grow.

parameterdescription.para: The key to look up or insert

simplesect.para: Reference to the value associated with key @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value & Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::At
memberdef.name: At

briefdescription.para: Access a value by key with an assertion on miss (non-const).

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to std::unordered_map::at() but calls DALI_ASSERT_ALWAYS instead of throwing std::out_of_range when the key is absent. Does NOT insert a default value for missing keys (unlike operator[]).

simplesect.para: Reference to the value associated with key @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: const Value & Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::At
memberdef.name: At

briefdescription.para: Access a value by key with an assertion on miss (const).

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to std::unordered_map::at() const but calls DALI_ASSERT_ALWAYS instead of throwing std::out_of_range when the key is absent.

simplesect.para: Const reference to the value associated with key @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: InsertResult Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::TryInsert
memberdef.name: TryInsert

briefdescription.para: Insert a key-value pair only if the key is absent (copy both).

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to std::unordered_map::insert(). Unlike Insert(), this never overwrites an existing value. If the key is already present, the existing value is left unchanged.
WARNING: Any pointer or iterator obtained before this call may be invalidated if the insert triggers a table grow.

simplesect.para: InsertResult::first — pointer to the value (existing or newly inserted) InsertResult::second — true if inserted, false if key already existed @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: InsertResult Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::TryInsert
memberdef.name: TryInsert

briefdescription.para: Insert a key-value pair only if the key is absent (move both).

simplesect.para: InsertResult @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: InsertResult Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::TryInsert
memberdef.name: TryInsert

briefdescription.para: Insert a key-value pair only if the key is absent (move key, copy value).

simplesect.para: InsertResult @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: InsertResult Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::TryInsert
memberdef.name: TryInsert

briefdescription.para: Insert a key-value pair only if the key is absent (copy key, move value).

simplesect.para: InsertResult @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Find
memberdef.name: Find

briefdescription.para: Find a value by key.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the value, or nullptr if not found @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: const Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Find
memberdef.name: Find

briefdescription.para: Find a value by key (const).

simplesect.para: Pointer to the value, or nullptr if not found @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: bool Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Erase
memberdef.name: Erase

briefdescription.para: Erase an entry by key.

simplesect.para: true if the key was found and erased, false otherwise @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Clear
memberdef.name: Clear

briefdescription.para: Mark all entries as empty.

detaileddescription.para: Every slot (OCCUPIED or TOMBSTONE) is reset to EMPTY and the size counters are zeroed. The table buffer itself is retained for reuse.
Key and Value objects in formerly-occupied slots are left in their current state: they will be overwritten when the slot is reused by a subsequent Insert, or destroyed when the table is freed.

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::ForEach
memberdef.name: ForEach

briefdescription.para: Call a functor for each live entry.

parameterdescription.para: Called with (const Key& key, Value& value) for each entry @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::ForEach
memberdef.name: ForEach

briefdescription.para: Call a functor for each live entry (const version).

parameterdescription.para: Called with (const Key& key, const Value& value) for each entry @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Size
memberdef.name: Size

simplesect.para: Number of live entries. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: bool Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Empty
memberdef.name: Empty

simplesect.para: true if there are no live entries. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Capacity
memberdef.name: Capacity

simplesect.para: Current table capacity (always a power of 2, or 0). @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: bool Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Contains
memberdef.name: Contains

briefdescription.para: Test whether a key exists in the map.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to std::unordered_map::contains() (C++20).

simplesect.para: true if the key is present, false otherwise @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Count
memberdef.name: Count

briefdescription.para: Return the number of entries with the given key (0 or 1).

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to std::unordered_map::count(). Since this is a map (not a multimap), the return value is always 0 or 1.

simplesect.para: 1 if the key is present, 0 otherwise @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Iterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::begin
memberdef.name: begin

briefdescription.para: Return an iterator to the first live entry.

detaileddescription.para: Iteration order is unspecified (depends on hash distribution). The iterator is invalidated by any operation that may grow or rehash the table. @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: Iterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::end
memberdef.name: end

briefdescription.para: Return a past-the-end iterator. @SINCE_2_5.20.

memberdef.definition: ConstIterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::begin
memberdef.name: begin

briefdescription.para: Return a const iterator to the first live entry. @SINCE_2_5.20.

memberdef.definition: ConstIterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::end
memberdef.name: end

briefdescription.para: Return a past-the-end const iterator. @SINCE_2_5.20.

memberdef.definition: ConstIterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::cbegin
memberdef.name: cbegin

briefdescription.para: Return a const iterator to the first live entry (explicit const). @SINCE_2_5.20.

memberdef.definition: ConstIterator Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::cend
memberdef.name: cend

briefdescription.para: Return a past-the-end const iterator (explicit const). @SINCE_2_5.20.

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Rehash
memberdef.name: Rehash

briefdescription.para: Resize the table to the smallest power-of-2 capacity that keeps load under 75%, clearing all tombstones in the process.

detaileddescription.para: If the input capacity is bigger than zero, the table will be resized to at least that capacity (rounded up to the next power of 2).

If the map is empty and targetCapacity is 0, the table is freed entirely (capacity becomes 0). This releases all Key/Value storage immediately via delete[], which is the recommended way to eagerly release resources after Clear(): map.Clear(); map.Rehash();//freesthetablebuffer,destructingallKey/Valueobjects
If the current capacity is already optimal, this is a no-op (unless there are tombstones, in which case an in-place rehash clears them).
Provides the strong exception guarantee: if RehashTo throws during the copy, the map is left unchanged.
WARNING: Invalidates all pointers, references, and iterators obtained before this call.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum desired capacity (0 to shrink to fit). @SINCE_2_5.20

memberdef.definition: uint32_t Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::HashIndex
memberdef.name: HashIndex

briefdescription.para: Hash a key and return a table index.

detaileddescription.para: Applies a finalizer (splitmix64-style) to the raw hash to improve bit distribution for linear probing with power-of-2 table sizes.

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::Grow
memberdef.name: Grow

memberdef.definition: void Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::RehashTo
memberdef.name: RehashTo

briefdescription.para: Rehash the table to a new capacity.

detaileddescription.para: Always copies (never moves) live entries into the new table so that the old table remains completely untouched until the full copy succeeds and the swap is committed.

Trade-off: rehash always performs O(n) copy work rather than O(n) move work for nothrow-movable types. This is acceptable because rehashing is amortised and infrequent, and correctness is not compromised.

If the application profile shows rehash to be a measurable hot path and all Key/Value types used are guaranteed nothrow-movable, this policy can be revisited by re-adding a MoveIfNoexcept path — but only after the old table has been fully abandoned (i.e. move everything first with no fallback, accepting the basic guarantee only).

memberdef.definition: Value * Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::InsertImpl
memberdef.name: InsertImpl

briefdescription.para: Unified insert implementation that forwards key/value perfectly.

memberdef.definition: InsertResult Dali::ManagedOpenHashMap< Key, Value, Hash, KeyEqual >::TryInsertImpl
memberdef.name: TryInsertImpl

briefdescription.para: Unified insert-or-ignore implementation.

detaileddescription.para: Like InsertImpl but never overwrites an existing value. Returns {ptr, true} on insertion, {ptr, false} if key was already present.

briefdescription.para: A managed open-addressed hash map with linear probing.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike OpenHashMap (which requires trivially-copyable/destructible types and uses calloc/memset for zero-overhead operation), ManagedOpenHashMap properly manages the lifecycle of its keys and values via constructors, destructors, and move semantics. This allows it to work with non-trivial types such as Dali::String.

Uses a single contiguous allocation (new Entry[]) for the entry table. Power-of-2 capacity, grows at 75% load. Deletion uses tombstones. Entry state is tracked via an explicit EntryState enum per slot.

Key requirements:
Must be default-constructible (required by Entry struct and new Entry[n]() in RehashTo/Grow).
Must be copyable and/or movable.
Must support comparison via KeyEqual.

Value requirements:
Must be default-constructible (required by Entry struct and new Entry[n]() in RehashTo/Grow).
Must be copyable and/or movable.

Note: the default-constructibility requirement exists because Entry holds Key and Value as plain members with default-initialisation (key{}, value{}), and RehashTo allocates new tables with new Entry[n]() which value-initialises every slot. Removing this constraint would require separating storage from construction (e.g. via placement new into raw memory), which is a more invasive change deferred for a future revision.

Resource release policy:
Erase() and Clear() do not reset key/value objects in the affected slots. Resources are released lazily: on slot reuse (Insert overwrites) or when the table is destroyed (delete[] destructs every Entry). To release resources eagerly after a Clear(), call Rehash() immediately afterwards — it frees the entire table buffer when size is zero.

Exception safety:
All operations provide at least the basic guarantee (no leaks, no dangling pointers).
Rehash/Grow provides the strong guarantee: if it throws, the map is unchanged.
Move constructor is noexcept conditional on Hash and KeyEqual being nothrow-move-constructible.
Move assignment is noexcept conditional on Hash and KeyEqual being nothrow-move-assignable.
RehashTo always copies (never moves) key/value into the new table. This unconditionally preserves the strong guarantee: if any copy throws, the old table is still fully intact and the new (partial) table is discarded. The cost is that rehash is always O(n) copy work rather than O(n) move work, but correctness is not compromised even for types whose move-assignment can throw.
IMPORTANT — differences from std::unordered_map:

listitem.para: Pointer/iterator stability. std::unordered_map guarantees that pointers and references to values remain valid as long as the element is not erased. THIS MAP DOES NOT. Any Insert() that triggers a grow reallocates the entire Entry[] array. Every Value* or Entry* obtained from a previous Find(), Insert(), operator[], At(), or iterator dereference is INVALIDATED by any subsequent insert that grows the table. Do not cache pointers or references across inserts.

Insert() always overwrites; std::unordered_map::insert() does not. In standard maps, insert({key, value}) is a no-op when the key already exists — only insert_or_assign() and operator[] overwrite. Here, Insert() always overwrites the existing value on collision. Use TryInsert() for insert-if-absent semantics that match std::unordered_map::insert().

No exception on missing key. std::unordered_map::at() throws std::out_of_range when the key is absent. At() in this map calls DALI_ASSERT_ALWAYS instead. operator[] inserts a default-constructed value for missing keys (same as std::unordered_map).

detaileddescription.para: API summary (std::unordered_map equivalents): map[key] -> operator[] (insert-or-get, overwrites) map.at(key) -> At() (assert on miss, no insert) map.insert({key, value}) -> TryInsert() (insert-if-absent only) map.insert_or_assign(k, v) -> Insert() (always overwrites) map.find(key) -> Find() (returns Value* or nullptr) map.contains(key) -> Contains() map.count(key) -> Count() map.erase(key) -> Erase() map.clear() -> Clear() for(auto& kv : map) -> range-for via begin()/end(), use kv.key()/kv.value()

parameterdescription.para: Hash functor: size_t operator()(const Key&) const
Equality functor: bool operator()(const Key&, const Key&) const @SINCE_2_5.20
